name: TabBar
purpose: Organizes and allows navigation between related groups of content at the same level of hierarchy.
whenToUse: To switch between different views within the same context or screen.
To organize closely related content into distinct sections.
As secondary navigation below an AppBar.
whenNotToUse: For top-level app navigation (use NavigationBar or NavigationRail instead).
When comparing content simultaneously (use a Grid or Split View).

accessibility.touchTargets: Ensure minimum touch target of 48x48dp for each tab.
accessibility.semantics: Provide clear semantics for selected state.
accessibility.keyboardNavigation: Support keyboard navigation (arrow keys to switch tabs).

flutterMappingNotes: Use DefaultTabController to wrap the Scaffold or the specific section containing the TabBar and TabBarView.
Variants: Set isScrollable to false for 'fixed' (equal width tabs) and true for 'scrollable' (variable width tabs).
Sizes: TabBar height is typically determined by its content and padding, rather than a fixed 48dp height, unless explicitly defined as a team standard.

States: Disabled tabs require custom product-level handling (e.g., ignoring taps and applying disabled text colors manually), as Flutter's TabBar does not have a built-in disabled state for individual tabs.
States: Use overlayColor (MaterialStateProperty) to define visual feedback for pressed and focus states.
Map item.selected.text to labelColor and labelStyle.
Map item.unselected.text to unselectedLabelColor and unselectedLabelStyle.
Map indicator.color to indicatorColor and indicator.height to indicatorWeight. Alternatively, use a custom Decoration for the indicator property for complex styling.
Use indicatorSize (TabBarIndicatorSize.tab or TabBarIndicatorSize.label) to control the width of the indicator.
Use tabAlignment (e.g., TabAlignment.start, TabAlignment.center) to control the alignment of tabs, especially when isScrollable is true.
